title: Network Security Standard
content: All network traffic must be encrypted using TLS 1.3. Open ports on production servers are strictly forbidden unless documented and approved by the CISO.

title: Access Control Policy
content: The principle of least privilege (PoLP) applies to all systems. User access reviews are conducted monthly for production environments.

title: Incident Response Plan
content: Security incidents must be reported to the SOC within 30 minutes of discovery. The IR team follows the SANS framework for containment and eradication.

title: Vulnerability Management
content: Critical vulnerabilities must be patched within 48 hours. High-severity issues must be addressed within 7 days. Monthly scanning is automated.

title: Secure Software Development Lifecycle (SSDLC)
content: Security reviews are mandatory at the design phase. Static (SAST) and dynamic (DAST) analysis tools must be integrated into the CI/CD pipeline.

title: BYOD Policy
content: Employees using personal devices for work must install MDM software. Corporate data must be kept in a separate encrypted container.

title: Data Retention and Disposal
content: Customer data is retained for 7 years unless a deletion request is received. Hard drives must be physically shredded before disposal.

title: Cloud Security Configuration
content: Publicly accessible S3 buckets are strictly prohibited. Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is mandatory for all cloud console access.

title: Encryption Key Management
content: Encryption keys must be rotated every 90 days. Keys are stored in a Hardware Security Module (HSM) or cloud-native KMS.

title: Third-Party Risk Assessment
content: All vendors with access to PII must undergo a security audit and sign a Data Processing Agreement (DPA). SOC 2 Type II reports are preferred.

title: Password Complexity Standards
content: Passwords must be at least 14 characters long and include three of the following: uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols.

title: Phishing Awareness Training
content: Monthly phishing simulations are conducted. Employees failing two consecutive tests must attend mandatory security re-training.

title: Data Classification Policy
content: Information is classified as Public, Internal, Confidential, or Highly Confidential. Labeling is mandatory for all documents.

title: Logging and Monitoring
content: All authentication attempts and administrative actions must be logged. Logs are forwarded to a central SIEM and retained for 365 days.

title: Physical Security Standards
content: Badge access is required for all office areas. Tailgating is a security violation. Visitors must be escorted at all times.

title: Secure Remote Access (VPN)
content: VPN access is restricted to company-managed laptops. Split tunneling is disabled to ensure all traffic passes through security stack.

title: Software Whitelisting
content: Only software from the approved corporate app store can be installed. Unauthorized software will be automatically uninstalled by MDM.

title: Disaster Recovery Testing
content: Full disaster recovery drills for critical systems are conducted bi-annually. RTO and RPO targets are reviewed annually.

title: Social Engineering Prevention
content: Support staff will never ask for your password. Verify identity through secondary channels for any unusual administrative requests.

title: API Security Policy
content: All external APIs must implement rate limiting and OAuth 2.0. API keys must not be hardcoded in client-side applications.

title: Workstation Lockdown
content: Computers must lock automatically after 5 minutes of inactivity. Screen protectors are required for employees in open-plan offices.

title: Shadow IT Disclosure
content: Usage of unapproved SaaS tools for business data is prohibited. Propose new tools through the IT Governance board.

title: Clean Desk Policy
content: Sensitive documents must be locked in drawers at the end of the day. Whiteboards in meeting rooms must be wiped after use.

title: Patch Management for IoT
content: IoT devices (cameras, sensors) must be on a separate VLAN. Default passwords must be changed before deployment.

title: Security Exception Process
content: Requests for deviations from security standards must be formally documented, risk-assessed, and signed off by the CIO.
